children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: From among the areas nominated for designation under this section, the appropriate Secretaries may designate empowerment zones and enterprise communities.

children.heading: Enterprise communities
children.label: 1 Enterprise communities
children.text: The appropriate Secretaries may designate in the aggregate 95 nominated areas as enterprise communities under this section, subject to the availability of eligible nominated areas. Of that number, not more than 65 may be designated in urban areas and not more than 30 may be designated in rural areas.

children.heading: Empowerment zones
children.label: 2 Empowerment zones
children.text: The appropriate Secretaries may designate in the aggregate 11 nominated areas as empowerment zones under this section, subject to the availability of eligible nominated areas. Of that number, not more than 8 may be designated in urban areas and not more than 3 may be designated in rural areas. If 8 empowerment zones are designated in urban areas, no less than 1 shall be designated in an urban area the most populous city of which has a population of 500,000 or less and no less than 1 shall be a nominated area which includes areas in 2 States and which has a population of 50,000 or less. The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development shall designate empowerment zones located in urban areas in such a manner that the aggregate population of all such zones does not exceed 1,000,000.

children.heading: Period designations may be made
children.label: c Period designations may be made
children.text: A designation may be made under subsection (a) only after 1993 and before 1996.

children.heading: Period for which designation is in effect
children.label: d Period for which designation is in effect
i
children.text: in the case of an empowerment zone, , orDecember 31, 2025

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an enterprise community, the close of the 10th calendar year beginning on or after such date of designation,

children.label: B
children.text: the termination date designated by the State and local governments as provided for in their nomination, or

children.label: C
children.text: the date the appropriate Secretary revokes the designation.

children.label: A
children.text: has modified the boundaries of the area, or

children.label: B
children.text: is not complying substantially with, or fails to make progress in achieving the benchmarks set forth in, the strategic plan under subsection (f)(2).
the area is nominated by 1 or more local governments and the State or States in which it is located for designation under this section,

children.label: A
children.text: to nominate the area for designation under this section, and

children.label: B
children.text: to provide the assurances described in paragraph (3),

such State or States and the local governments provide written assurances satisfactory to the appropriate Secretary that the strategic plan described in the application under subsection (f)(2) for such area will be implemented,
the appropriate Secretary determines that any information furnished is reasonably accurate, and

such State or States and local governments certify that no portion of the area nominated is already included in an empowerment zone or in an enterprise community or in an area otherwise nominated to be designated under this section.
demonstrates that the nominated area satisfies the eligibility criteria described in section 1392,

children.label: A
children.text: describes the coordinated economic, human, community, and physical development plan and related activities proposed for the nominated area,

children.label: B
children.text: describes the process by which the affected community is a full partner in the process of developing and implementing the plan and the extent to which local institutions and organizations have contributed to the planning process,

children.label: C
children.text: identifies the amount of State, local, and private resources that will be available in the nominated area and the private/public partnerships to be used, which may include participation by, and cooperation with, universities, medical centers, and other private and public entities,

children.label: D
children.text: identifies the funding requested under any Federal program in support of the proposed economic, human, community, and physical development and related activities,

children.label: E
children.text: identifies baselines, methods, and benchmarks for measuring the success of carrying out the strategic plan, including the extent to which poor persons and families will be empowered to become economically self-sufficient, and

children.label: i
children.text: the establishment of the new branch, affiliate, or subsidiary will not result in a decrease in employment in the area of original location or in any other area where the existing business entity conducts business operations, and

children.label: ii
children.text: there is no reason to believe that the new branch, affiliate, or subsidiary is being established with the intention of closing down the operations of the existing business entity in the area of its original location or in any other area where the existing business entity conducts business operation, and
includes such other information as may be required by the appropriate Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In addition to the areas designated under subsection (a), the appropriate Secretaries may designate in the aggregate an additional 20 nominated areas as empowerment zones under this section, subject to the availability of eligible nominated areas. Of that number, not more than 15 may be designated in urban areas and not more than 5 may be designated in rural areas.

children.heading: Period designations may be made and take effect
children.label: 2 Period designations may be made and take effect
children.text: A designation may be made under this subsection after the date of the enactment of this subsection and before .January 1, 1999

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A nominated area shall be eligible for designation under this subsection only if the poverty rate for each population census tract within the nominated area is not less than 20 percent and the poverty rate for at least 90 percent of the population census tracts within the nominated area is not less than 25 percent.

children.heading: Treatment of census tracts with small populations
children.label: ii Treatment of census tracts with small populations
I
children.text: more than 75 percent of such tract is zoned for commercial or industrial use, and

children.label: II
children.text: such tract is contiguous to 1 or more other population census tracts which have a poverty rate of not less than 25 percent (determined without regard to this clause).

children.heading: Exception for developable sites
children.label: iii Exception for developable sites
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to up to 3 noncontiguous parcels in a nominated area which may be developed for commercial or industrial purposes. The aggregate area of noncontiguous parcels to which the preceding sentence applies with respect to any nominated area shall not exceed 2,000 acres.

children.heading: Certain provisions not to apply
children.label: iv Certain provisions not to apply
children.text: Section 1392(a)(4) (and so much of paragraphs (1) and (2) of section 1392(b) as relate to section 1392(a)(4)) shall not apply to an area nominated for designation under this subsection.

children.heading: Special rule for rural empowerment zone
children.label: v Special rule for rural empowerment zone
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture may designate not more than 1 empowerment zone in a rural area without regard to clause (i) if such area satisfies emigration criteria specified by the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The parcels described in subparagraph (A)(iii) shall not be taken into account in determining whether the requirement of subparagraph (A) or (B) of section 1392(a)(3) is met.

children.heading: Special rule for rural areas
children.label: ii Special rule for rural areas
children.text: If a population census tract (or equivalent division under section 1392(b)(4)) in a rural area exceeds 1,000 square miles or includes a substantial amount of land owned by the Federal, State, or local government, the nominated area may exclude such excess square mileage or governmentally owned land and the exclusion of that area will not be treated as violating the continuous boundary requirement of section 1392(a)(3)(B).

children.heading: Aggregate population limitation
children.label: C Aggregate population limitation
children.text: The aggregate population limitation under the last sentence of subsection (b)(2) shall not apply to a designation under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Previously designated enterprise communities may be included
children.label: D Previously designated enterprise communities may be included
children.text: Subsection (e)(5) shall not apply to any enterprise community designated under subsection (a) that is also nominated for designation under this subsection.

children.heading: Indian reservations may be nominated
children.label: E Indian reservations may be nominated

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Section 1393(a)(4) shall not apply to an area nominated for designation under this subsection.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: ii Special rule
children.text: An area in an Indian reservation shall be treated as nominated by a State and a local government if it is nominated by the reservation governing body (as determined by the Secretary of the Interior).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In addition to the areas designated under subsections (a) and (g), the appropriate Secretaries may designate in the aggregate an additional 9 nominated areas as empowerment zones under this section, subject to the availability of eligible nominated areas. Of that number, not more than seven may be designated in urban areas and not more than 2 may be designated in rural areas.

children.heading: Period designations may be made and take effect
children.label: 2 Period designations may be made and take effect
children.text: A designation may be made under this subsection after the date of the enactment of this subsection and before .January 1, 2002

children.heading: Modifications to eligibility criteria, etc.
children.label: 3 Modifications to eligibility criteria, etc.
children.text: The rules of subsection (g)(3) shall apply to designations under this subsection.

children.heading: Empowerment zones which become renewal communities
children.label: 4 Empowerment zones which become renewal communities
children.text: The number of areas which may be designated as empowerment zones under this subsection shall be increased by 1 for each area which ceases to be an empowerment zone by reason of section 1400E(e). Each additional area designated by reason of the preceding sentence shall have the same urban or rural character as the area it is replacing.1See References in Text note below.1
